title: BS in Computer Science and Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
text:
BS in Computer Science and Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
BS in Computer Science and Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
Apply now
Request info
The BS in Computer Science and Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology combined major (Khoury College and Bouvé College of Health Sciences) is designed to facilitate critical thinking, information literacy, and oral and written communication skills. In this program, students have an opportunity to focus on the fundamentals of program design, software development, computer organization, systems and networks, theories of computation, principles of languages, and advanced algorithms and data.
This program is new for Fall 2024.
Explore program requirements in the course catalog
Program details
Research opportunities
Research opportunities
You will have opportunities to partner with leading professors on active research projects. Here are a few of our research areas:
Natural language processing and information retrieval
Human-computer interaction
Data science
Explore research at Khoury College
Learning outcomes
Learning outcomes
In this program, you’ll learn to:
Apply mathematical foundations, algorithmic principles, and computer science theory in the modeling and design of systems in a way that demonstrates comprehension of the tradeoffs involved in design choices.
Apply design principles in the construction of software systems of varying complexity.
Use current techniques, skills, and tools necessary for effective and secure computing practice.
Campus location
Boston
Located in the heart of Boston, Northeastern’s oldest campus is home to a vibrant community of students, researchers, and faculty working across disciplines to build a better world through CS.
Explore Boston's campus

Degree costs
Degree costs
Pursuing a world-class education is an investment in your future. It’s also one of the most important decisions you’ll ever make. Visit our Undergraduate Admissions site to find current Northeastern University costs. You can also estimate the cost of your degree using our College Cost Estimator or Net Price Calculator.
Find and estimate degree costs
Financial aid and scholarships
Financial aid and scholarships
Northeastern University offers a range of financial aid and scholarship opportunities to help you fund your education. You’ll receive an initial financial aid package that outlines your options after admission. Once you enroll, Khoury College has multiple scholarship opportunities for current students to continue supporting your studies. We also encourage all students to submit the FAFSA to determine your eligibility for federal aid and loans.
Learn more about scholarship opportunities
Have questions?
Have questions?
Let us know if you have any questions about the application or enrollment process. We are here to help!
Contact an enrollment counselor
Faculty
Get to know the engaged, experienced professors who are here to support you every step of the way.
Meet our faculty
Matthew Goodwin
Matthew Goodwin is a professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the Bouvé College of Health Sciences. He works with people on the autism spectrum to develop and evaluate behavioral assessment and intervention technologies, enabling caregivers to more capably and compassionately assist their loved ones.
Read bio
Visit the Bouvé College of Health Sciences
The BS in Computer Science and Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology is an interdisciplinary collaboration with the Bouvé College of Health Sciences.
Learn about the Bouvé College of Health Sciences
Get a head start on your graduate degree
In our accelerated master’s programs, you can start earning your master’s degree while you are still pursuing your undergraduate studies.
Browse accelerated BS+MS programs
